appliedUpdates.description: Added monetization architecture notes to .factory/library/architecture.md for public /spikes project access behavior, Stripe webhook no-op semantics for missing customer IDs, downgrade cleanup pattern, idempotency sequencing, and standardized upgrade CTA URL.
Added STRIPE_PRO_PRICE_ID to required Worker environment variables in .factory/library/environment.md.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the allowed exception pattern for public reads on otherwise protected endpoints (example: GET /spikes allowed without auth only when scoped by project query param).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: cross-area-integration introduced conditional auth bypass in worker/src/index.ts for project-scoped pulls.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit webhook guidance for two recurring Stripe patterns: (1) return 200 no-op for events missing customer IDs and (2) implement downgrade handlers as tier update + Pro-feature cleanup in one flow.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: stripe-webhook-completeness uses both patterns in webhooks.ts and tests validate them.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen requirement that every new/changed CLI command path includes corresponding automated tests, even when endpoint tests already exist in worker repo.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: usage-tracking implemented usage.rs behavior without dedicated CLI tests while other CLI features in mission generally included command-level tests.

rejectedObservations.observation: Extract helper functions from cross-area-flows.test.ts into test/helpers.ts.
rejectedObservations.reason: non-systemic refactor suggestion; no current correctness or validation risk

rejectedObservations.observation: Centralize duplicated fetch_usage helper between usage.rs and shares.rs immediately.
rejectedObservations.reason: ambiguous scope for scrutiny pass/fail; duplication is non-blocking and can be handled in a follow-up implementation feature

rejectedObservations.observation: Worker-backend skill was followed correctly for limit-enforcement-upgrade.
